title: Vatica lanceolata
intro: Vatica lanceolata, also known as the Malabar ironwood or Malabar forest tree, is a species of tree in the family Dipterocarpaceae. The health benefits of Vatica lanceolata include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Vatica lanceolata include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Vatica lanceolata is a species of tree in the family Dipterocarpaceae. It belongs to the genus Vatica, which consists of approximately 40 species of trees. The family Dipterocarpaceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the 'Dipterocarps' or 'Borneo Ironwood family'. The Dipterocarpaceae family is part of the order Malvales, which includes other flowering plant families. This classification places Vatica lanceolata within the broader context of flowering plant taxonomy.
